title: Grilled Glazed Kenyan Chicken Kebabs
description: Smoky and springy chicken kebabs glazed with a sweet, anti-inflammatory peanut and coconut sauce, served hot on a stone plate for a monsoon-spiced Kenyan feast.

ingredients.name: Chicken breast, skinless, boneless, cut into 1.5 inch cubes
Red bell pepper, seeded, cut into 1.5 inch cubes

instructions: Preheat grill to medium-high heat (about 200°C) and oil the grates.
Thread 1 chicken cube, 1 bell pepper cube, and 1 onion wedge onto each soaked skewer, alternating ingredients.
In a small saucepan, whisk together honey, peanut butter, coconut milk, lime juice, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper until smooth; simmer until slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.
Brush each skewer with olive oil and the glaze, reserving some glaze for later.
Grill skewers for 5-6 minutes per side, basting with glaze, until chicken reaches internal temperature of 75°C and vegetables are charred.
While grilling, heat the remaining glaze in a small pan over low heat until it coats the back of a spoon.
After removing from grill, brush each kebab with the thickened glaze and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Serve hot on a preheated hot‑stone plate to keep the kebabs sizzling.
Enjoy immediately with a side of steamed millet or roasted plantains.
